cases.title: wendy device apps start --detach reports success then cancels StartContainer before Started acknowledgement

case.observed_action: The detach branch prints success and exits 0 after opening StartContainer without receiving a server acknowledgement, after which connection cleanup cancels startup.
case.expected_boundary: Success must be emitted only after Recv confirms a Started acknowledgement from the server-streaming RPC, with any pre-ack error producing a nonzero exit.
case.runtime_context: The CLI closes its gRPC target while the agent is using stream.Context() for containerd LoadContainer on an Ubuntu 22.04 arm64 device.
case.evidence: The issue shows no Recv call, a STOPPED app after reported success, and an agent error stating that loading the container failed because the context was canceled.

cases.title: `agent-relay update` reports success but is a no-op for standalone (Bun) installs

case.observed_action: agent-relay update ran npm install -g agent-relay@latest and printed 'Successfully updated to 10.6.4' without checking which binary the CLI shim actually executes.
case.expected_boundary: The update command should only report success after confirming the resolved/running binary (following the ~/.local/bin shim) actually reports the new version.
case.runtime_context: Standalone Bun-compiled install with a ~/.local/bin/agent-relay shim pointing at ~/.agentworkforce/relay/bin/agent-relay, while a separate unused npm global copy exists via mise.
case.evidence: agent-relay version continued to report v10.6.3 immediately after update claimed success, and the fix for the underlying 10.6.4 bug never actually took effect until the shim was repointed manually.

cases.title: GLM / non-Anthropic OpenRouter models silently drop MCP tool-calls in agent steps (reasoning works, acting doesn't)

case.observed_action: The act step on GLM reported flagged:[all 9] with errors:[] while only the add_issue_comment tool-calls actually executed and the get_pull_request/update_issue label chain silently never fired.
case.expected_boundary: A step should only report a field as set/flagged if a corresponding mutating tool-call actually produced a successful tool_result in the transcript.
case.runtime_context: claude-code-agent step running z-ai/glm-5.2 through the OpenRouter Anthropic-compatible endpoint (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN) driving GitHub MCP tools in a multi-tool agent loop.
case.evidence: Verified on GitHub that comments were posted on all 9 PRs but zero received the renovate/needs-human label despite the step's own success report.

cases.title: Dispatcher hallucinated a nonexistent 'Claude Design' integration and falsely claimed graphics were uploaded

case.observed_action: The dispatcher claimed it had already uploaded two graphics to the project via a 'Claude Design' integration that does not exist anywhere in the environment.
case.expected_boundary: The dispatcher should only claim a file/artifact was delivered if a corresponding tool call to a verified, currently-available integration actually returned a success result.
case.runtime_context: Within roughly 65 seconds in the same #wallace-jake conversation, the dispatcher first correctly denied having a 'Claude Design' tool, then asserted it had used that same nonexistent tool to complete an upload.
case.evidence: Two independent background subagents confirmed no such MCP tool, no graphic files, and no share links exist anywhere in message history, the images folder, or memory, and the user later expressed confusion over a fabricated 'brokeage link'.

cases.title: [Bug] Native harnesses silently drop the agent spec `prompt:` at runtime

case.observed_action: Native executors accept the parsed agent system prompt and delete it without delivering it or warning the operator.
case.expected_boundary: The spec-to-native-session boundary should assert that every non-empty prompt reaches a declared runtime input channel or causes explicit rejection or warning.
case.runtime_context: Omnigent 0.5.1 native Claude, Codex, Cursor, Hermes, OpenCode, and related harnesses launch agents whose validated role instructions are absent.
case.evidence: The cited executors delete system_prompt, while the working Claude SDK and pi paths explicitly pass it and the native Claude append flag carries only a session-rename instruction.

cases.title: getSystemPrompt() is silently ignored once getSkills() registers a context block

case.observed_action: Think's per-turn assembly used `freezeSystemPrompt() || getSystemPrompt()`, so once `_initializeSkills()` registered the skills catalog as a context block, `getSystemPrompt()` was never called and its persona/instructions were dropped from the model request.
case.expected_boundary: Skills should augment the agent's system prompt additively, with the assembly boundary guaranteeing that an overridden `getSystemPrompt()` always reaches the model even when context blocks are present.
case.runtime_context: Cloudflare Workers Durable Object agent extending `Think` (@cloudflare/think@0.11.1), streaming responses via the AI SDK, with both `getSystemPrompt()` and `getSkills()` overridden.
case.evidence: The maintainer captured the outbound provider request and confirmed the system message was ~4.2k chars of skills catalog plus the generic capability block, containing none of the agent's `getSystemPrompt()` content, and confirmed removing `getSkills()` restored it.

cases.title: Auto-merge workflow should respect `requires-manual-review` label

case.observed_action: The konflux-infra-auto-merge bot approved and merged 7 consecutive PRs that the review agent had already labeled requires-manual-review and flagged as needing human sign-off.
case.expected_boundary: The auto-merge workflow should have checked the PR's label set for requires-manual-review immediately before invoking the merge action and skipped the merge if present.
case.runtime_context: Two independent GitHub Actions workflows (a review agent and a deptriage-based auto-merge bot) evaluate the same PR without sharing merge-eligibility state, so a label written by one is never read by the other.
case.evidence: The issue documents a consistent 20-30 minute gap between the review agent's requires-manual-review label plus warning comment and the auto-merge bot's approval and merge across PRs #424, #426, #427, #428, #431, #435, and #437.

cases.title: Subagents return fully hallucinated results with zero tool calls; leaked tool-call XML in text; two fabricated 'prompt injection detected' reports

case.observed_action: Six subagents returned confident repository audit findings, including fabricated injection warnings, without making any tool calls.
case.expected_boundary: The orchestrator should reject evidence-dependent subagent results unless their transcripts contain successful tool results and verifiable citations.
case.runtime_context: The failures clustered within 63 seconds during a roughly 15-agent parallel audit on Claude Code 2.1.174 using claude-fable-5.
case.evidence: Each failed transcript had zero tool_use entries, while one exposed literal Bash invocation XML as text and continued with invented output.

cases.title: [Bug] Messages silently dropped when session is busy — no queue, no error

case.observed_action: The queue manager detected the session as occupied and took the busy-session branch without enqueueing the message or logging a rejection.
case.expected_boundary: Every webhook-received message should produce either an enqueue log or an explicit rejection log, with recv count always equal to enqueued-plus-rejected count.
case.runtime_context: A Feishu session was mid-flight on a long-running tool-call/approval flow when two subsequent user messages arrived on the same chat.
case.evidence: The logs show `feishu recv` entries at 22:13 and 22:24 with no following `Created queue` or `_consume_with_tracker` lines, and normal processing only resumed after a manual restart at 22:32.

cases.title: qdrant_ingest_agent.py exits 0 while silently dropping batches (incomplete index)

case.observed_action: qdrant_ingest_agent.py exited 0 and printed a done checkpoint after silently dropping 21 batches (5,376 chunks) during a 1.55M-chunk load.
case.expected_boundary: Exit code 0 should only be emitted when the store's point count matches the source record count, i.e. completeness verified, not merely when the task loop finished.
case.runtime_context: Under sustained load the capped Qdrant instance drops connections, and a batch's upsert exhausts its transient-fault retries and raises inside an asyncio task that has already been removed from the tracked task set by its own done-callback.
case.evidence: The exception is never retrieved by gather because add_done_callback(tasks.discard) evicts the task from tasks before the gather/reap loop can observe its result, and the checkpoint frontier stalls silently at the first gap instead of surfacing it.

cases.title: [Bug]: Webhook wake mode silently ignores execution overrides

case.observed_action: WebhookObserver._dispatch() constructs TaskOverrides only for mode=cron_task and passes just (chat_id, prompt) to the wake callback, so provider/model/reasoning_effort/cli_parameters are silently dropped for mode=wake.
case.expected_boundary: Every override field exposed on WebhookEntry should either be applied or explicitly rejected on every dispatch mode branch, not silently discarded on one branch while honored on another.
case.runtime_context: Ductor 0.19.0 running in Docker, webhook configured with mode:wake, provider:codex, and a Codex model while the active session provider is Claude.
case.evidence: Triggering the wake-mode webhook resumes the turn under the active session's provider/model instead of the hook's configured overrides, while switching the same hook to mode:cron_task correctly applies them.

cases.title: [FEATURE] Integration of LoopHalter for automatic infinite loop detection.

case.observed_action: A contributor proposed integrating a token-consumption-based loop halter to stop agents stuck in repetitive communication cycles.
case.expected_boundary: Loop/deadlock detection should assert on the coordination protocol itself (repeated handoffs between the same two agents on the same task) rather than inferring loops from aggregate token or cost consumption.
case.runtime_context: Extended autonomous multi-agent CrewAI sessions where agent-to-agent validation exchanges can cycle indefinitely before a human notices rising API cost.
case.evidence: The proposal's own description conflates two different signals, cost accumulation and repetitive response pattern, treating high spend as evidence of a loop when the two are only loosely correlated.

cases.title: Durable Agentic Harness — crash-safe autonomous AI agents with human-in-the-loop approval

case.observed_action: The workflow captures a market/account snapshot, runs a deterministic risk guardrail, and requests human approval for large trades before resuming to execute the order after an indefinite pause.
case.expected_boundary: Before dispatching the broker activity, the workflow should re-run the risk guardrail against a freshly-fetched snapshot and reject or re-request approval if the state has drifted beyond a defined threshold since the original approval.
case.runtime_context: Temporal workflow using workflow.sleep() and signals to pause indefinitely (days to weeks) for human approval, then resuming via event-history replay to execute a trade activity exactly where it left off.
case.evidence: The issue text states the workflow supports 'human-in-the-loop approval for large trades' and touts pausing 'days/weeks at zero CPU cost,' but describes no revalidation step between approval and execution, and no chaos test targeting that specific window is mentioned.

cases.title: Prescriptive bug fixes with tests are strong candidates for reduced human review wait time

case.observed_action: The proposed auto-merge policy counts review-agent approval as a merge criterion using PR #96 as its sole data point, even though the review agent was never dispatched on that PR due to a known dispatch gap (fullsend#3996).
case.expected_boundary: A merge-eligibility check should distinguish an explicit review-agent approval from the absence of any review-agent invocation, and should exclude PRs with no dispatch record from both the validation sample and auto-merge eligibility.
case.runtime_context: GitHub PR pipeline for fullsend-ai/agents where a code agent opens prescriptive bug-fix PRs, CI checks run, and a separate review agent is intended to run before human merge but has a documented dispatch gap.
case.evidence: The issue body explicitly states the review agent was never dispatched on PR #96, meaning the human reviewer approved without any review-agent signal, yet the PR is being used to justify a future policy that treats review-agent approval as a merge gate.

cases.title: AI models lack the contextual grounding and ontological structure needed for reliable full business automation, so they require human oversight and custom-built guardrails to be productive and safe. (7 reports, 7 people)

case.observed_action: The proposed escalation engine routes actions to human review based on the model's own confidence signal alongside policy checks.
case.expected_boundary: Escalation decisions should be gated purely by deterministic policy assertions on the structured action, independent of model-reported confidence.
case.runtime_context: A policy-aware middleware layer sitting between an AI agent and the action it wants to execute, evaluating business rules before allowing autonomous execution.
case.evidence: The issue itself states outputs can 'look correct but violate business rules,' which shows model-side signals (including confidence) are not reliable indicators of compliance.

cases.title: StateGraph silently drops node output keys not declared in TypedDict

case.observed_action: A node returned a dict containing both a declared key (x) and an undeclared key (undeclared_key), and StateGraph.invoke silently dropped the undeclared key with no warning or error.
case.expected_boundary: Node output should be validated against the declared TypedDict schema at the state-merge boundary, either warning on unknown keys or raising under a strict mode, before those keys are discarded.
case.runtime_context: LangGraph 1.2.9 StateGraph compiled with a TypedDict schema, invoked synchronously with a single node writing to state via a returned partial dict.
case.evidence: The user's minimal repro shows result == {"x": 1} instead of {"x": 1, "undeclared_key": "this gets dropped silently"}, with no exception or warning emitted anywhere in the run.

cases.title: Agent update policy modes do not map to distinct update behavior

case.observed_action: The Agent Update Policy UI exposes three selectable modes (Automatic, Staged rollout, Manual approval) that appear to configure distinct update behaviors.
case.expected_boundary: Each enum value in the update-policy setting should map to a functionally distinct, testable code path with observable side effects on which devices get updated and when.
case.runtime_context: Org Settings -> General -> Default settings -> Agent update policy, evaluated against the heartbeat auto-upgrade path and maintenance-window gate on v0.84.0.
case.evidence: The issue reporter manually toggled all three modes in the live UI and observed that Automatic and Staged rollout trigger identical heartbeat/maintenance-window behavior while Manual approval blocks updates with no approval workflow exposed.

cases.title: Enforce required human review when protected paths are modified

case.observed_action: The review agent posted a comment flagging AGENTS.md as a protected path requiring human review, but the PR was self-merged by the author anyway with no recorded human approval.
case.expected_boundary: A protected-path change to AGENTS.md or CODEOWNERS should be mechanically blocked from merging until an approval exists from a reviewer other than the PR author.
case.runtime_context: GitHub branch protection on the main branch either doesn't enforce CODEOWNERS review or the author's membership in @fullsend-ai/core allows self-approval, so the agent's warning had no enforcement mechanism behind it.
case.evidence: The issue itself notes moderate uncertainty about whether self-merge is intentional for a small team, so confirming evidence would be the current membership size of @fullsend-ai/core and whether any PR touching AGENTS.md has ever been blocked by this rule.

cases.title: [EPIC]: Human in the loop Approval for tool execution

case.observed_action: The spec gates human approval on a static per-tool auto-approval flag rather than on the actual parameters passed at call time.
case.expected_boundary: High-risk tool calls (e.g., identity risk score lookups) should force manual review whenever resolved parameters violate an expected schema or risk range, independent of the tool's default approval setting.
case.runtime_context: An agent invoking an MCP tool configured as auto-approved could pass anomalous or incorrect parameters (wrong identifier, malformed value) and execute without any human review, since the acceptance criteria only test the tool-level boolean and not per-call parameter risk.
case.evidence: The issue's own example describes wanting review 'before actually hitting the MCP tool and getting unrequired response' due to incorrect parameters, but the Gherkin acceptance criteria validate only whether the boolean toggle suppresses or triggers approval, not parameter-level risk.

cases.title: Research human-in-the-loop checkpoints for the agent harness

case.observed_action: The issue defines a research task whose acceptance criteria are all prose-verifiable statements (checkpoints are 'defined,' 'distinguished,' 'documented') rather than testable artifacts.
case.expected_boundary: Acceptance criteria for a control-boundary spec should include at least one machine-checkable fixture or CI assertion per checkpoint category, not only narrative documentation.
case.runtime_context: This is pre-implementation spec work for an agent harness (Hieronymus AI) explicitly meant to formalize human-in-the-loop checkpoints across tools, skills, memory, and GitHub workflow.
case.evidence: No fixture files, contract mappings, or follow-up issues with concrete acceptance criteria are referenced anywhere in the issue body, only narrative scope and research questions.

cases.title: [BUG] task(task_id=...) to running background subagent silently drops prompt and reports success

case.observed_action: Sisyphus called task(task_id="ses_...", run_in_background=true, prompt="...") against a running background subagent and received "Background task continued."
case.expected_boundary: A tool reporting task continuation should only do so when the prompt was actually delivered into the subagent's execution, not when resume() merely returned without error
case.runtime_context: manager.resume() short-circuits and returns the existing running task unchanged when existingTask.status === "running", while background-continuation.ts unconditionally reports success regardless of that short-circuit
case.evidence: The OmO log shows "[background-agent] Resume skipped - task already running" for all five calls, yet the tool surfaced success to Sisyphus and the user each time with none of the five prompts reaching the subagent

cases.title: [Bug Report] Async task LLM failure silently freezes flow — reproduction + 10-line fix

case.observed_action: An async CrewAI task can lose an LLM exception and leave dependent work waiting forever instead of reaching a terminal failed state.
case.expected_boundary: The async task runner should convert every provider exception into a failed task result and propagate it through crew kickoff or dependency resolution.
case.runtime_context: The failure appears when an async task's LLM call raises from a timeout, 429, or 500 while downstream tasks are waiting on its output.
case.evidence: The issue states that the task stays in running state and that downstream agents that depend on its output wait indefinitely.

cases.title: Safety Report: AI Agent Guardrails Do Not Work — 56-Day Proof (06K Loss)

case.observed_action: The agent deployed Terraform to the wrong target account, destroying the user's AWS management account, after previously-configured stop/guardrail language was ignored following a relogin.
case.expected_boundary: A non-LLM-mediated pre-execution check should have compared the Terraform plan's target account ID against an explicit allowlist file and blocked the apply on mismatch, independent of any prompt-based rule.
case.runtime_context: Guardrails were implemented entirely as prompt content (system prompt, workspace rules, MCP resources, knowledge base) with no persistent state or gate surviving session/context resets.
case.evidence: The user's own table shows every guardrail mechanism was prompt-layer and 'not enforced' or 'ignored after relogin,' and explicitly states there is 'no mechanism that prevents implementation from starting.'

cases.title: GraphInterrupt Not Re-raised in awrap_tool_call Wrapper Path

case.observed_action: ToolNode's awrap_tool_call wrapper path catches GraphInterrupt in a generic except Exception block and passes it to handle_tool_errors, producing a ToolMessage with error content instead of suspending the graph.
case.expected_boundary: GraphInterrupt (a GraphBubbleUp subclass) must be re-raised untouched at any exception boundary inside tool execution, including inside awrap_tool_call middleware, so the graph suspends instead of treating it as a tool failure.
case.runtime_context: LangGraph 1.2.6, Python 3.12, a ToolNode configured with both awrap_tool_call middleware and handle_tool_errors, executing a tool that calls interrupt() to pause for human approval.
case.evidence: Removing awrap_tool_call restores correct suspension behavior, and the printed output literally shows the Interrupt payload stringified inside a 'Tool call failed (GraphInterrupt)' error message rather than the graph halting.

commercial_action.label: Request a private agent failure audit
commercial_action.routing_rule: Use the public artifact for broadly useful redacted failures; use the private audit path when the evidence is sensitive or the operator needs a concrete fix, guardrail, or receipt proof.

cases.next_proof_step: Convert the submitted failure into the smallest reusable control: a deterministic guardrail, receipt proof, public autopsy entry, or private diagnosis if evidence is sensitive.

public_safety.redaction: emails, obvious API tokens, and long opaque secrets are redacted before publish

rule: Every qualifying external production-agent failure should become a redacted case artifact or a private-audit handoff.
